What is the relevance of Angiosperms to evolution?
mcqs.answer: Angiosperms are significant to evolution as they represent the most diverse group of terrestrial plants, contributing to the complexity of ecosystems and interactions with pollinators and other organisms.
distractors: Angiosperms primarily evolved to thrive in aquatic environments, reducing their relevance to terrestrial ecosystems.
The evolutionary significance of Angiosperms lies in their ability to reproduce without pollinators, relying solely on wind and water.
Angiosperms are known for their role in the development of carboniferous forests, which predates their actual appearance in the fossil record.
